reviews.review_description: If you’re an older individual that enjoys scrolling on FaceBook I guess you will enjoy this. However, if you’re a part of the more popular age group then you won’t have a need for this app. Personally, I’m just waiting for them to add live-streaming so I can stream to social media.
I wish I could talk to my girlfriend on video like we. Do on our phone, I wish for the day to do that in VR.

reviews.review_title: Nice but I already had shortcuts
reviews.review_description: We need a VR app... Not shortcuts. I wanna interact with my feed with my hands... Swipe up what I dislike, swipe down that I like... I dont know... Something else than this that's for sure.

My main issue with this app, along with, instagram, YouTube, and really any streaming app. Is that we can’t sign in through this mobile app. I’m sorry I have a randomly generated 25 character long password. But I really don’t feel like taking the headset on and off every few characters to see if that letter was supposed to be capital or not. 

Otherwise it works just like the desktop tab and that’s good enough for me.

reviews.review_title: Please pay attention to Thailand. Please
reviews.review_description: I want to have Thai language I want to be able to video chat with friends without removing the VR. I have group video chats with friends every day. It makes me feel not lonely even though I'm far away. Thank you.
Trash that is trying to force people to  give up there 1st amendment rights

As a user of the Oculus Quest 2, I was excited to try out the Facebook app and see how it compared to using the website on a regular computer or mobile device.

One of the first things I noticed was the autocorrect feature. While it can be helpful at times, I found it to be a bit overbearing and often changed my words to something completely different. This made it frustrating to enter posts or comments, and I often had to spend extra time correcting the autocorrect mistakes.

I also had some trouble signing in to the app. As someone who uses a password manager with randomised passwords, I had to look at my computer screen through the headset to get the password and then reposition the headset after memorizing parts of it. It would be helpful if the app offered an option to sign in with the Facebook account that is already linked to the headset, to make the process easier.

Lastly, I was disappointed that the app simply launched the Facebook mobile site in an app browser window, rather than offering a true VR experience. While I understand that it is still in beta, I was hoping for something more interactive and immersive. I will update my review if and when the app is updated in the future.

--

The Oculus Quest 2 has a wide range of single player games, as well as many co-op multiplayer games that can be enjoyed with friends and loved ones, even if you're not in the same location. VR technology brings people together in a unique and exciting way. If you want to purchase additional headsets for family members, loved ones, or friends, you can use the €30 credit available through the coupon code link (https://www.oculus.com/referrals/link/xinwix/) to get a discount. This credit can be used to purchase at least 1-3 games or co-op games to get started, making it a great opportunity to share the virtual reality experience with others.

reviews.review_title: Should be able to take 3d photos/videos
reviews.review_description: Utilizing the stereo camera on the headset, it is possible to take 3D photos and videos. It is even possible to capture the environment as 360 assets and share online to be used as the home environment or virtual theater apps. They will become new kinds of media someday, and Meta can be the first one to make it happen.

Downloaded to my q3 headset and tried it for the first time. Then app completely disappeared. Tried checking App Store and it says I already purchased the app. Tried to launch via my phone app but to no avail and it froze my headset.
This is the best, i can talk to my friend in canada.one problem tho i cant be in a call and play a game at the same time because the person in the call cant hear me

I am finding it hard to sign in. It may be easy for a regular person to sign in, however, I use a password manager with randomised passwords; I have to look at my computer screen through the nose hole of the quest headset to get the password and have to reposition it after I memorise parts of it.

It would be nice if it could automatically sign you in or at least give you an option to sign in with the facebook account your headset is already linked to.
Nothing to see here. Just the old 2D staff. Nothing new.

I don't usually use the Meta  Quest facebook app often as the mobile experience is far superior, although the layout is clean and I have yet to run into any "issues" as such, the odd times I do opt to use it I often find the experience is lacking and leaves a lot to be desired which can often be frustrating.

For example, I recently recorded some gameplay in VR and wondered to myself if I could share this with a friend and maybe add to my story, which you expect would be a seamless event.

But again I was quite dumbfounded that for whatever reason video media can't be uploaded direct from the device to Facebook, instead having to opt for the long-winded process of having to sync media to the Quest app and then share from there?

A very apple-esque way of doing things..

It would also be great for the UI to follow your system preferences for dark mode, as the visual aesthetics can be quite jarring.

For now mobile would be my preferred choice, but it would be great to see some improvements on the Quest application in the future with these features implemented.
Love the app and the problem is that the hand tracking is not working
